rationale: Both candidates converge on identical, highly specific fix details (sanitize_min_tokens_stop_ids in vllm_omni/worker/sampling_utils.py, same runner call sites, same test file), which is strong evidence of real grounding rather than fabrication, and both confirm the reporter's own root-cause diagnosis was correct — though neither hedges against the sparse ground truth, which only shows a maintainer tagging colleagues for review (PTAL) rather than confirming a merged fix, so both are somewhat overconfident in declaring the issue closed/resolved. X pulls ahead on completeness: it explicitly engages with and rebuts the reporter's two suggested fixes with reasoning, ties the fix back to the reporter's exact reported version, and is formatted as a clean, postable maintainer comment. Y is substantively similar (and adds a nice concrete touch — the bundled qwen3_tts.yaml already shipping min_tokens:2 with a `# see #4962` comment) but its output leaks internal pipeline/report scaffolding ('# Run report', 'draft_answer', 'Linked issues/PRs' bookkeeping) rather than reading as a real maintainer response, and it doesn't address the reporter's own suggested fixes directly.
